reasoning: Kadyrov has a well-documented history of publicly claiming he wants to step down or take an "indefinite leave" (e.g., in 2016, 2017, and September 2022). In every past instance, these statements were political theater designed to elicit public pleas for him to stay and to reaffirm his value to the Kremlin, and he inevitably retracted them within days. While recent (2023-2024) rumors regarding his failing health (such as chronic kidney failure) add some credibility to the possibility of a genuine resignation this time around, the forecast horizon is extremely short. The knowledge cutoff is May 12, 2025, and the resolution date is June 1, 2025 — a window of just 19 days. Even if a succession plan is being legitimately activated due to health or political reasons, a formal removal/resignation within this exact three-week window is improbable unless he dies or becomes completely incapacitated. Therefore, relying heavily on the base rate of his previous "boy who cried wolf" stunts and the brief timeframe, the probability is very low.
